meaning: The progress of a country in terms of economic growth, quality of life and access to resources.
The total value of goods and services produced by a country in a year.
A measure of development that combines income, education and life expectancy.
A country with a low GDP per capita and a limited standard of living.
A country with a high GDP per capita and a high standard of living.
A country that is rapidly industrialising and experiencing economic growth, e.g. China or Brazil.
The difference in levels of development between the world's richest and poorest countries.
A company that operates in more than one country and has a global reach.
Investment made by a foreign company or government into the economy of another country.
The process by which the world is becoming increasingly interconnected through trade, culture and technology.
A trading partnership that aims to achieve greater equity in international trade and improve conditions for producers in developing countries.
Money, food, skills or technology given by one country to another to help its development.
Aid given directly from one country to another.
Aid given by several countries through an international organisation such as the UN or World Bank.
Technology that is suited to the needs and skills of people in developing countries, using local materials.
The number of live births per 1000 people per year.
The number of deaths per 1000 people per year.
The number of babies who die before their first birthday, per 1000 live births.
The average number of years a person is expected to live.
The percentage of adults who can read and write in a population.
